body.summary: We consider this governance action to be constitutional.
body.rationaleStatement: This proposal is a Treasury Withdrawal Governance Action to withdraw ₳4,600,000 from the Cardano Treasury in support of Harmonic Laboratories’ proposal titled “Pebble & Ecosystem maintenance: TypeScript core of Cardano.” In accordance with Article 2, Section 6, Paragraphs 1 and 2 of the Cardano Constitution, this proposal employs immutable off-chain references using IPFS. Furthermore, the proposal text includes an overview, rationale, technical background, budget, milestones, management structure, audit and reporting framework, risks, and a Constitutional Self-Assessment; we therefore determine that it presents sufficient information to be evaluated as a Governance Action. Regarding Article 2, Section 7, Paragraph 1, this proposal aims to develop the smart contract language “Pebble” and maintain the Cardano TypeScript ecosystem. It details a 12-month implementation period, budget breakdown, milestone-based fund releases, return conditions, failsafe sweep, contingency reserve, and other related matters. It also explains specific deliverables and acceptance criteria, including smart contract tooling for TypeScript, hard fork support, benchmarks, IDE tooling, documentation, and tutorials. Regarding Article 2, Section 7, Paragraph 2, it is stated that Harmonic Labs has not received ADA from the Cardano Treasury within the past 24 months. Regarding Article 2, Section 7, Paragraph 3, the requested amount for this proposal is ₳4,600,000, which falls within the 350M ADA Net Change Limit applicable to Epochs 613–713 at the time of submission. Furthermore, the requested amount for this proposal is stated in ADA, and the proposal uses a conversion rate of 0.25 ADA/USD for budget estimation. Regarding Article 2, Section 7, Paragraphs 4 and 5, the proposal details an independent oversight committee, independent financial audits, quarterly reporting, public transaction records, measurable adoption metrics, milestone verification, and suspension/resumption authority. It also explains the treasury escrow structure using SundaeSwap treasury contracts, as well as independent audits by TxPipe and MLabs. Furthermore, Article 2, Section 7, Paragraph 6 details the use of “treasury.ak” and “vendor.ak” for separately managed treasury escrow contracts, delegation to the predefined always_abstain voting option, non-delegation to an SPO, and auditable on-chain transaction records. This structure is intended to align with the requirements regarding “one or more separate accounts that can be audited by the Cardano Community,” “not be delegated to an SPO,” and “must be delegated to the predefined abstain voting option.” Therefore, as no clear conflict with the Cardano Constitution has been identified, we determine that this proposal is constitutional.
body.counterargumentDiscussion: The recipient address is also used in another proposal by the same proposer (gov_action1guz68e8zkwphcdc8wnp40cclkv92qgnel7xnffmsmp2ljp09qtwqq596k4c), and it is difficult to say that this necessarily meets the requirement for a “separate account” as stipulated in Article 2, Section 7, Paragraph 6 of the Constitution. In light of this, some of our members considered the proposal unconstitutional.
body.conclusion: For the reasons stated above, we determine that it is constitutional.
